weekly_summary: First week back — building aerobic base with controlled volume. Focus is on easy effort and consistency, not speed.
coaching_notes: Your last 14 days show solid aerobic volume (48 km across 5 runs) with average HR well in Zone 2. VO2 max holding at 54 — good base. No fatigue flags. Ready to add one quality session this week.
next_week_preview: If this week goes well, we'll introduce a longer tempo interval and bump long run to 20 km.

days.title: Full Recovery
days.description: Complete rest. Prioritize 8+ hours sleep and good nutrition. Light walking is fine.

days.title: Easy Aerobic Base
days.description: Conversational pace the whole way. If you can't speak in full sentences, slow down. Nasal breathing is a good indicator.
days.notes: Run by HR (130–145 bpm), not pace. It should feel almost too easy.

days.title: Threshold Intervals
days.description: Warm up 15 min easy → 3 × 10 min at threshold (comfortably hard, ~5–6 words per breath) with 3 min easy jog recovery → cool down 10 min easy.
days.notes: Target pace: ~4:15–4:30/km. Don't go out too hard on interval 1.

days.title: Recovery Jog
days.description: Very easy effort. Purpose is active recovery from Wednesday's intervals — flush lactate, stay loose.
days.notes: If legs feel heavy, shorten to 5 km or skip entirely.

days.title: Rest Day
days.description: Full rest. Prepare mentally and physically for the weekend runs.

days.title: Medium Long Run
days.description: Steady easy effort from start to finish. Bring water or run a loop near a fountain. Fuel if going past 75 min.
days.notes: Keep HR under 150 bpm. This is your second-longest run of the week.

days.title: Long Run with Marathon Finish
days.description: Easy for first 13 km, then last 5 km at marathon goal pace (~4:45–5:00/km). Practice your race fueling — gel at 45 min and 80 min.
days.notes: Don't skip the gel practice. Race day fueling needs to be trained.
